seoTitle: TDK News | Product Updates and Industry Insights
seoDescription: Latest news from TDK including new product releases, technology updates, and industry trends in passive components and power electronics.

articles.title: TDK Expands MLCC Portfolio with New High-Capacitance Series
articles.summary: TDK announces expansion of multilayer ceramic capacitor portfolio with new high-capacitance series targeting automotive and industrial applications.
articles.content:
TDK Corporation has announced the expansion of its multilayer ceramic capacitor (MLCC) portfolio with the introduction of new high-capacitance series designed specifically for demanding automotive and industrial applications. The new CGA series automotive-grade capacitors feature X8R dielectric technology, enabling operation at temperatures up to 150°C while maintaining stable capacitance characteristics.

The expanded portfolio includes capacitance values up to 100µF in compact 1210 packages, addressing the growing demand for high-density energy storage in electric vehicle powertrains and advanced driver assistance systems (ADAS). These capacitors are qualified to AEC-Q200 Rev. E standards and manufactured under IATF 16949 quality management systems.

Key features of the new series include:
- Operating temperature range: -55°C to +150°C
- Voltage ratings from 6.3V to 100V DC
- Capacitance range: 1µF to 100µF
- Available in 0805, 1206, and 1210 package sizes
- RoHS compliant and halogen-free

The new series is particularly suited for decoupling applications in high-temperature environments such as engine control units, transmission control modules, and LED lighting systems. TDK's advanced thin-layer technology enables industry-leading capacitance density while maintaining reliability under harsh automotive conditions.

LiTong Electronics, as an authorized TDK distributor, will stock the new series and provide technical support for customer designs. Samples and production quantities are available now with standard lead times.
articles.author: TDK Corporation

articles.title: TDK Film Capacitors Power Next-Generation Solar Inverters
articles.summary: TDK's B3277x series film capacitors selected by leading solar inverter manufacturers for 1500V DC applications.
articles.content:
Leading solar inverter manufacturers have selected TDK's B3277x series DC link film capacitors for their next-generation 1500V DC string inverters. The high-voltage film capacitors provide the reliability and performance required for utility-scale solar installations, where system availability and maintenance costs are critical considerations.

The B3277x series offers voltage ratings up to 1500V DC with capacitance values from 1µF to 10µF, meeting the requirements of modern high-power solar inverters. The metallized polypropylene construction provides self-healing properties, ensuring continued operation even under abnormal voltage stress conditions.

Key advantages for solar applications:
- 100,000+ hour operational lifetime
- Self-healing properties for enhanced reliability
- Low ESL for high-frequency switching
- High ripple current capability
- Wide operating temperature range (-40°C to +105°C)

With the solar industry moving toward higher DC bus voltages (1500V vs 1000V) to reduce system costs and improve efficiency, TDK's high-voltage film capacitors provide a proven solution. The capacitors have passed extensive qualification testing including temperature cycling, humidity exposure, and accelerated life testing.

LiTong Electronics provides technical support for solar inverter designers, including DC link capacitor sizing calculations and thermal analysis. Custom configurations are available for specific application requirements.
articles.author: LiTong Technical Team

articles.title: TDK Introduces High-Current Power Inductors for EV Charging Infrastructure
articles.summary: New SLF series power inductors deliver up to 50A current handling for DC fast charging applications.
articles.content:
TDK has introduced a new series of high-current power inductors designed specifically for electric vehicle charging infrastructure. The expanded SLF series now includes inductors rated for continuous currents up to 50A, addressing the requirements of DC fast chargers delivering 150kW to 350kW output power.

The new inductors feature metal composite cores that provide soft saturation characteristics, making them ideal for the transient load conditions typical of EV charging applications. The shielded construction minimizes EMI radiation, helping charger manufacturers meet stringent electromagnetic compatibility requirements.

Series highlights:
- Current ratings: 10A to 50A continuous
- Inductance range: 1µH to 100µH
- Low DCR for high efficiency
- Shielded construction for low EMI
- Operating temperature: -40°C to +125°C

The inductors are designed for the PFC (power factor correction) and DC-DC converter stages of EV chargers, where high efficiency and reliability are essential. The metal composite core material provides better performance under overload conditions compared to traditional ferrite cores.

TDK's new series complements its existing portfolio of passive components for EV charging, including high-voltage film capacitors, aluminum electrolytic capacitors, and common mode chokes. Complete BOM solutions are available through LiTong Electronics.
articles.author: TDK Product Marketing

articles.title: TDK Expands AEC-Q200 Qualified Product Portfolio
articles.summary: Additional ceramic capacitors and inductors receive AEC-Q200 qualification for automotive applications.
articles.content:
TDK Corporation has announced the expansion of its AEC-Q200 qualified product portfolio, with additional ceramic capacitors and power inductors now qualified for automotive applications. The expanded qualification covers over 500 new part numbers across multiple product families.

Newly qualified products include:

**Ceramic Capacitors:**
- Extended capacitance range in CGA series
- Additional voltage ratings up to 500V
- New package sizes including 0603 and 1210
- X8R dielectric for 150°C operation

**Power Inductors:**
- SLF series expanded with automotive variants
- Current ratings from 0.5A to 15A
- Shielded and unshielded options
- Metal composite and ferrite core options

All qualified products undergo rigorous testing per AEC-Q200 Rev. E requirements, including:
- Temperature cycling (-55°C to +125°C or +150°C)
- Mechanical shock and vibration
- High-temperature operational life
- Humidity resistance
- Solderability and terminal strength

The expanded portfolio enables automotive designers to source a wider range of passive components with the quality and reliability required for modern vehicles. From infotainment systems to EV powertrains, TDK's qualified components provide the foundation for reliable automotive electronics.

LiTong Electronics maintains inventory of popular AEC-Q200 qualified parts and provides PPAP documentation support for automotive customers.
articles.author: TDK Automotive Division

articles.title: TDK Announces New Sustainability Initiatives for Passive Components
articles.summary: TDK commits to carbon-neutral manufacturing and expands halogen-free product portfolio.
articles.content:
TDK Corporation has announced comprehensive sustainability initiatives aimed at reducing the environmental impact of its passive component manufacturing operations. The company has committed to achieving carbon-neutral manufacturing at all production facilities by 2035, with interim targets of 50% reduction by 2030.

Key sustainability initiatives include:

**Manufacturing:**
- Transition to renewable energy sources
- Implementation of energy-efficient production equipment
- Waste reduction and recycling programs
- Water conservation measures

**Product Design:**
- Expansion of halogen-free product portfolio
- Reduction of hazardous substances
- Design for recyclability
- Lifecycle assessment integration

**Supply Chain:**
- Responsible sourcing of raw materials
- Supplier sustainability audits
- Reduction of packaging materials
- Optimized logistics to reduce transportation emissions

TDK's MLCC and aluminum electrolytic capacitor product lines are already transitioning to halogen-free formulations, with over 90% of new products meeting halogen-free requirements. The company is also working to eliminate conflict minerals from its supply chain through comprehensive supplier verification.

For customers, these initiatives mean continued access to high-quality passive components with reduced environmental impact. TDK's sustainability commitment aligns with the growing demand for environmentally responsible electronics manufacturing.

LiTong Electronics supports TDK's sustainability initiatives and can provide documentation on halogen-free and RoHS-compliant products for customer environmental compliance requirements.
articles.author: TDK Corporate Communications

articles.title: TDK Components Enable Next-Generation Industrial IoT Devices
articles.summary: Compact passive components from TDK support the miniaturization and performance requirements of industrial IoT applications.
articles.content:
The industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) is driving demand for compact, high-performance passive components that can operate reliably in harsh industrial environments. TDK's comprehensive portfolio of MLCCs, inductors, and sensors is enabling the next generation of connected industrial devices.

**Key Component Solutions:**

**Ultra-Small MLCCs:**
- 008004 size (0.25mm x 0.125mm) for wearable sensors
- High capacitance density in compact packages
- Low ESL for high-frequency decoupling
- Extended temperature ratings

**Compact Power Inductors:**
- 0603 and 0805 sizes for space-constrained designs
- High efficiency for battery-powered devices
- Shielded options for EMI-sensitive applications
- Metal composite cores for improved saturation

**EMI Suppression Components:**
- Common mode chokes for data line filtering
- Ferrite beads for high-frequency noise suppression
- Integrated filter solutions

Industrial IoT applications present unique challenges including wide temperature ranges, high vibration, and long operational lifetimes. TDK's industrial-grade components are designed to meet these requirements while enabling the miniaturization needed for compact sensor nodes and edge devices.

Application examples:
- Predictive maintenance sensors
- Wireless vibration monitors
- Smart motor controllers
- Environmental monitoring systems
- Asset tracking devices

LiTong Electronics provides component selection support for IIoT designers, helping optimize designs for size, power consumption, and reliability. Reference designs and evaluation kits are available for common IIoT applications.
articles.author: LiTong Industrial Team
